name: Norway
avoid: July — peak domestic tourism, crowded fjords and higher prices
Mountain roads (e.g. Eagle Road, Trollstigen) close Oct–May
Plan for rain in Bergen — notoriously wet year-round
Northern Lights need clear skies — have a backup night booked

cities.name: Oslo
cities.notes: Viking Ship Museum, Vigeland Sculpture Park, Akershus fortress and fjord

cities.name: Bergen
cities.notes: Bryggen UNESCO wharf, Funicular to Floyen and gateway to fjords

cities.name: Tromso
cities.notes: Northern lights capital, Midnight Sun and Arctic Cathedral

cities.name: Flam
cities.notes: Flam Railway — world's steepest rail journey into Naeroyfjord

cities.name: Alesund
cities.notes: Art Nouveau archipelago town and gateway to Geirangerfjord cruise

cities.name: Voss
cities.notes: Adventure capital — skiing Jan–Easter, paragliding and white-water rafting in summer

cities.name: Geirangerfjord
cities.notes: UNESCO fjord, Seven Sisters waterfall, Eagle Road hairpins and 4–5 hr cruise

cities.name: Gudvangen
cities.notes: Viking village Njardarheimr at the end of Nærøyfjord. Gateway from Flam ferry.

cities.name: Loen
cities.notes: Nordfjord base — Loen Skylift, Briksdal glacier, turquoise lake Lovatnet.

cities.name: Trollstigen
cities.notes: 11-hairpin mountain pass with dramatic viewpoint platform. Road closed Oct–May.

cities.name: Lofoten
cities.notes: Arctic archipelago — dramatic peaks, fishing villages, midnight sun, and early aurora. Reine, Henningsvær, Å.

itinerary.sim: Telenor or Telia — buy at Oslo Airport (Gardermoen)
apps: Yr (Norway weather — far more accurate than Google)
Aurora Alerts + Hello Aurora (KP index for Northern Lights)

activities.name: Vigeland Sculpture Park — 212 bronze & granite works across 80 acres, free all day
Akershus Fortress — free grounds, medieval ramparts, Oslo harbour panorama
Oslo Opera House — free rooftop walk on the marble-clad sloping roof
Aker Brygge waterfront — fresh shrimp sandwich, harbour atmosphere, evening bars
Karl Johans Gate — pedestrian street from Central Station to Royal Palace
National Museum — Munch's The Scream original + Norway's largest art collection
Ferry Aker Brygge → Bygdøy (10 min, seasonal)
Viking Age Museum (Bygdøy) — 1,200-yr-old Oseberg & Gokstad ships
Fram Museum — polar ship that reached both poles, walk inside the hull
Holmenkollen ski jump viewpoint + Ski Museum (T-bane line 1)
Oslofjord sightseeing cruise (2 hrs, summer)
Sørenga sea pool — outdoor fjord swimming on floating pontoon, free (summer)
Munch Museum — 28,000 works, Deichman Library rooftop views
Grünerløkka neighbourhood — vintage shops, murals, Oslo's best coffee

Bergen.name: Bergen
Bergen.note: Take the Bergen Line scenic train from Oslo (7 hrs) — one of Europe's great rail journeys; book at vy.no 90 days out for Mini Pris fares. Bergen is Europe's rainiest city — waterproofs are non-negotiable.

activities.name: Bryggen Wharf — UNESCO medieval Hanseatic warehouses; explore the hidden back alleys before 10 AM (cruise ships arrive later)
Hanseatic Museum in Bryggen — merchant life preserved since 1704
Bergen Fish Market (Fisketorget) — fresh shrimp sandwich or salmon cone, best lunch in Bergen
Fløibanen funicular to Mt. Fløyen (320m) — 360° city, fjord & island views
Bergenhus Fortress & Håkon's Hall — free exterior, medieval harbour walk
Hardangerfjord or Mostraumen fjord cruise from Bergen (summer, full day)
Troldhaugen — Edvard Grieg's lakeside villa, museum & summer concerts
Ulriken cable car — Bergen's highest point (643m), optional hike across ridge to Fløyen (2 hrs)
KODE Art Museums — Munch, Picasso & world-class silver collection
Gamle Bergen Museum — open-air 18th-century neighbourhood of 55 wooden houses
Fantoft Stave Church — 900-yr-old stave church rebuilt after arson, 5-min drive south
Vøringsfossen waterfall day trip to Eidfjord — one of Norway's tallest falls (182m), 3 hrs drive summer only

Flam.name: Flam
Flam.note: From Bergen: train to Voss (1 hr, vy.no) + bus to Flam (1.5 hrs). Arrive in the evening for Day 1 (Stegastein + village); Day 2 is the Railway + Nærøyfjord cruise. Book Flåmsbana at norwaysbest.com — summer dates sell out 2–3 months ahead.

activities.name: Stegastein viewpoint — cantilevered platform 650m above Aurlandsfjord, 15-min drive from village. Free, open 24/7. Best at golden hour.
Undredal village (20-min drive) — Norway's smallest village (~100 people), smoked brown goat cheese tasting, free entry
Ægir Bryggeri brewpub — Viking-longhouse design, local craft beers & hearty Norwegian food
Flam waterfront walk along Aurlandsfjord at dusk
Flåm Railway (Flåmsbana) to Myrdal & return — world's steepest standard-gauge rail, 865m climb, Kjosfossen waterfall performance stop
activities.tip: Book at norwaysbest.com. Sit left side heading up for best views. Summer fills 2+ months out.

activities.name: Nærøyfjord UNESCO electric ferry Flam → Gudvangen (2 hrs) — world's narrowest fjord, sheer 1,700m cliffs, silent electric vessel
activities.tip: Book combined Rail + Fjord at norwaysbest.com. One-way cruise; Bus 950 continues from Gudvangen.

activities.name: Bus 950 Gudvangen → Voss (1 hr, Skyss) — continue onward toward Alesund or stay in Voss
Aurlandsfjellet Snow Road — drive to Aurland viewpoint on Norway's highest mountain road (summer only, free)

Gudvangen.name: Gudvangen
Gudvangen.note: Arrive by Nærøyfjord ferry from Flam. The Viking village Njardarheimr is the main attraction — a living reconstruction of a Viking-era settlement. Half-day stop before continuing to Bergen via Voss.

activities.name: Njardarheimr Viking Village — living Viking settlement with costumed inhabitants, axe throwing, archery, mead tasting, and craftsman workshops
activities.tip: Open May–Sept. Allow 2–3 hrs. Book at vikingvalley.no

activities.name: Nærøyfjord arrival — UNESCO's narrowest fjord, sheer 1,700m cliffs on both sides, arrive by electric ferry from Flam
Gudvangen village walk — small fjord-head settlement beneath dramatic peaks, quiet after cruise ships leave
Stalheimskleiva scenic road to Voss — 13 hairpin bends down a dramatic gorge (summer only, one-way downhill)

hotels.name: Stay in Voss instead (30 min drive) for more options

Loen.name: Loen
Loen.note: Nordfjord gem between Bergen and Alesund. Loen Skylift is one of Norway's most spectacular cable cars. Drive from Bergen (4 hrs) or Alesund (2.5 hrs). Briksdal Glacier arm is an easy walk. Lake Lovatnet is surreally turquoise in summer.

activities.name: Loen Skylift to Mt Hoven (1,011m) — 5-min cable car ride to panoramic fjord & glacier views, Via Ferrata option at summit
activities.tip: Book at loenskylift.no. Go early morning for clearest views. Restaurant Hoven at top.

activities.name: Lake Lovatnet drive — surreal turquoise glacial lake framed by green peaks; stop at multiple viewpoints along the road
Sande viewpoint — classic Nordfjord panorama, free roadside stop
Loen village walk — tiny fjordside settlement with wooden houses and mountain backdrop
Briksdal Glacier (Briksdalsbreen) — arm of Jostedalsbreen, Europe's largest mainland glacier; 45-min walk from car park to glacier face
activities.cost: Free (trolley car ₹1,500 pp RT if preferred)
activities.tip: Wear good shoes. Glacier retreating fast — still impressive but go soon.

activities.name: Olden village — charming fjord-head settlement, cruise ship port in summer, quiet in Sept
Kjenndalen glacier valley hike — less crowded alternative to Briksdal (2 hrs RT)
Drive to Trollstigen or Alesund (2.5 hrs via Rv15 + E136)

Trollstigen.name: Trollstigen
Trollstigen.note: The Troll's Ladder — 11 hairpin bends climbing 858m with a dramatic cantilevered viewpoint platform. Road open late May–Oct only (check vegvesen.no). Best as a day stop between Loen/Geirangerfjord and Alesund. Drive self or join a bus tour.

activities.name: Trollstigen mountain pass — drive 11 hairpin bends with 10% gradient; stop at Stigfossen waterfall (320m drop right beside the road)
Trollstigen Visitor Centre — award-winning cantilevered glass platform overlooking the serpentine road and valley below (free)
Trollveggen (Troll Wall) — Europe's tallest vertical rock face (1,100m); viewpoint 15-min detour on Rv63 heading toward Åndalsnes
Gudbrandsjuvet gorge — narrow canyon with walkways and cascading rapids, 30-min drive south on Rv63 (free)

hotels.name: Drive through — best as a transit stop between Geirangerfjord and Alesund

Lofoten.name: Lofoten
Lofoten.note: Arctic archipelago 200km north of the Arctic Circle. Fly to Leknes (LKN) or Svolvær (SVJ) from Tromsø or Oslo. Rent a car — essential for island-hopping. Sept: dark enough for aurora, still mild (5–10°C), fall colors. Traditional rorbuer (fisherman cabins) are the quintessential stay. Book 2–3 months ahead for Sept.

activities.name: Svolvær harbour walk — gateway to Lofoten, dramatic Svolværgeita peak backdrop, galleries & cafés
Magic Ice gallery — Lofoten ice sculpture exhibition inside a frozen chamber
Henningsvær (30-min drive) — 'Venice of Lofoten', fishing village on tiny islands connected by bridges; famous football pitch
Henningsvær galleries & Kaviar Factory — contemporary art in former fish factory
Evening aurora hunt from Svolvær beach — KP 2+ sufficient, dark from 9 PM in Sept
Drive E10 south — one of the world's most scenic roads; mountains, beaches, bridges between islands
Reine — postcard-perfect red rorbuer beneath Reinebringen peak; photograph from the bridge
Reinebringen hike — 1,560 stone steps to 448m with jaw-dropping panorama of Reine and islands (2 hrs RT)
activities.tip: Start early. Steep but well-maintained Sherpa steps.

activities.name: Å (pronounced 'Oh') — end of the road; Stockfish Museum + traditional fishing village preserved from 1800s
Hamnøy fishing village — tiny bridge-connected village, classic photo spot at sunrise/sunset
Ramberg or Haukland Beach — white sand, turquoise Arctic water, dramatic mountain backdrop (free)
Lofotr Viking Museum at Borg — full-size reconstructed Viking chieftain's longhouse (83m), boat rides, period activities
Nusfjord fishing village — UNESCO-listed, one of Norway's best-preserved fishing villages, charming harbour
Northern Lights from Uttakleiv Beach or Haukland — iconic aurora reflections in shallow tidal water, KP 2+ in Sept
Sea eagle RIB safari from Svolvær — white-tailed eagles swoop for fish beside the boat (2–3 hrs)
Trollfjorden boat trip — narrow 2km fjord with sheer 1,100m walls, only accessible by boat
Kabelvåg — old trading post, Lofoten Cathedral (wooden, 1898), quiet harbour walk
Flight to Tromsø from Svolvær or Leknes (45 min)

Voss.name: Voss
Voss.note: Transit hub between Bergen Line and Alesund. Don't skip Stalheim Gorge — one of Norway's most dramatic road descents (14 hairpins). Ski season Jan–Easter. Adventure centre summer: rafting, paragliding, skydiving.

activities.name: Voss Gondol cable car to 886m summit — fjord & mountain panorama (free to hike down)
Paragliding tandem from Hangur summit (summer)
White-water rafting on Vosso river (June–Sept)
Voss ski resort — groomed runs + off-piste (Jan–March)
Skydiving over Voss (Norway's skydive capital, June–Sept)
Stalheim Hotel viewpoint & Stalheimskleiva — 13% gradient, 13 hairpin bends into the gorge (summer road only, check opening dates)
Nærøydalen valley walk — glacier-carved valley below Stalheim, flat easy path, free
Tvindefossen waterfall (26m drop, roadside, free) — 15-min north of Voss on E16
Voss town church (Vangskyrkja) — 13th-century stone church, free
Voss Museum of Folk Life

Alesund.name: Alesund
Alesund.note: Best May–Sept. Geirangerfjord roads (Eagle Road, Trollstigen, Dalsnibba) close Oct–May — confirm openings at vegvesen.no. Rent a car in Alesund for Day 2 self-drive loop; all major agencies at the airport.

activities.name: Aksla Hill viewpoint — 418 steps, panoramic archipelago & city view (free, best at sunset)
Art Nouveau Centre (Jugendstilsenteret) — why the city rebuilt in Jugendstil after the 1904 fire
Sunnmøre Museum — Viking ships & 55-building open-air museum on the water
Ålesund Fish Market & harbour walk — local fiskesuppe (fish soup) lunch
Atlanterhavsparken aquarium — Atlantic sea life including wolf fish & seals

days.theme: Eagle Road Self-Drive Loop to Geirangerfjord

activities.name: Drive Eagle Road (Ørnesvingen) — 11 hairpin bends to viewpoint 620m above Geirangerfjord; best light before 10 AM
Dalsnibba mountain plateau — drive Norway's highest public road to 1,476m (summer only, ₹100 toll)
Flydalsjuvet cliff viewpoint — classic postcard overhang shot over the fjord (free)
Geiranger village — explore waterfront; Seven Sisters & Suitor waterfalls visible from shore
Geiranger → Hellesylt ferry (45 min across the fjord, then drive back to Alesund 1.5 hrs)
activities.tip: Much faster return than retracing Eagle Road. Ferry runs summer only.

Geirangerfjord.name: Geirangerfjord
Geirangerfjord.note: Best May–Sept. All approach roads (Eagle Road, Trollstigen, Rv63) close Oct–May. Recommended as an overnight from Alesund for longer trips. Cruise season peaks June–Aug; kayak season May–Sept.

activities.name: Geirangerfjord sightseeing cruise — Seven Sisters (82m) & Suitor waterfalls, Friaren (Pulpit)
activities.tip: Full 4-hr cruise to Hellesylt and back ₹7,500 pp — book at geirangerfjord.no

activities.name: Eagle Road (Ørnesvingen) — 11-hairpin viewpoint 620m above the fjord (car essential)
Flydalsjuvet cliff viewpoint — classic postcard overhang (free, 5-min drive from village)
Dalsnibba mountain plateau drive to 1,476m — highest public road in Norway (summer only, ₹100 toll)
Geiranger village waterfront walk — charming 250-person village at fjord head

hotels.name: Hotel Union Geiranger (iconic clifftop, fjord view)

activities.name: Guided fjord kayaking — paddle beneath Seven Sisters waterfall (3–4 hrs)
activities.tip: Book with Geiranger Kayaks. Small groups, morning slots sunniest.

activities.name: Hike to Skageflå mountain farm — abandoned farm 250m above water, viewpoint into the fjord (3 hrs RT)
Westerås Farm viewpoint — lesser-known clifftop view of full fjord, short drive from village
Return to Alesund via Trollstigen (2.5 hrs) — stop at Trollstigen visitor centre platform

Tromso.name: Tromso
Tromso.note: Best Sept–March for Northern Lights. Feb–March: daylight returns + snow stable — ideal combination. Polar night (no sun at all) Nov–Jan. Summer (June–Aug): Midnight Sun, whale watching closes, dog sledding unavailable.

activities.name: Arctic Cathedral (Ishavskatedralen) — iconic triangular A-frame glass facade (free exterior; interior ₹800 pp)
Fjellheisen cable car — 421m summit, panoramic fjord & city views year-round
Polaria Museum — interactive Arctic exhibits & bearded seal feeding shows
Tromsø Arctic-Alpine Botanical Garden — world's northernmost botanical garden, free (summer)
Storgata street, Ølhallen pub (Norway's oldest) & cosy café with brunost (brown cheese)
Guided Northern Lights evening tour — minibus to dark fjord valleys 40–80 km away (4–5 hrs)
activities.tip: Book with 2–3 operators (Pukka Travels, Lights Over Lapland, Northern Lights Tromso) — most offer free rebook night if no lights appear

activities.name: KP index 2+ is sufficient away from city lights; KP 3+ is excellent — check Hello Aurora app
Self-drive option: rent a car, drive E8 or E6 toward Skibotn — no guide cost, full flexibility
Camera settings: ISO 1600–3200, f/2.8, 8–20s shutter, manual focus infinity
Dog sledding with Husky teams — 2–3 hrs in Arctic wilderness (Nov–March)
activities.tip: Arctic Adventure Tours, Tromsø Villmarkssenter — book 3–6 months ahead in peak Feb–March

activities.name: Reindeer sleigh ride + Sami cultural camp, joik performance & lávvu dinner (Nov–March)
Snowmobile safari across frozen tundra (Nov–March)
Ice fishing on frozen Arctic lake — included in many winter safari packages
Whale watching boat tour — orcas & humpbacks migrate Oct–Jan into fjords near Tromsø
activities.tip: Best Oct–Dec; Brim Explorer or Whale Safari operator

activities.name: Sommarøy island day trip (summer) — Arctic white-sand beaches & crystal-clear turquoise fjord, 1 hr drive
Tromsø University Museum — Arctic archaeology, Sami culture & aurora science exhibit
Midnight Sun boat tour (June–July) — sun never sets; fjord dinner cruise

connections.method: Bergen Line scenic train (7 hrs)
Train to Myrdal + Flåm Railway descent (5.5 hrs)
Nærøyfjord electric ferry (2 hrs, UNESCO fjord)
Bus to Voss (1 hr) + train to Bergen (1.5 hrs)
Bus 950 via Stalheimskleiva gorge (1 hr)
Train to Myrdal + Flåm Railway descent (3 hrs)
Train direct Bergen Line (1.5 hrs)
Drive via E39 + Rv15 (4 hrs) or bus with change
Express bus Nor-Way (4 hrs) or flight (45 min)
Drive via Rv15 + E136 (2.5 hrs)
Drive via Rv15 + E39 (2.5 hrs)
Drive Rv63 via Ørnesvingen Eagle Road (1.5 hrs)
Drive via Åndalsnes + E136 (1.5 hrs)
Ferry Geiranger→Hellesylt + drive (2.5 hrs)
Drive via Eagle Road (90 min, summer only)
Flight via Bodø or Tromsø (3–5 hrs with change)
Flight direct (1 hr, Vigra Airport)
Flight Svolvær/Leknes to Tromsø (45 min)
Flight to Svolvær or Leknes (45 min)
Flight direct (2 hrs)
Flight direct (2 hrs)
Flight to Leknes via Bodø (4 hrs with change)
Ferry Nærøyfjord to Gudvangen + Bus 950 to Voss
Bus or drive via E16 + Rv13 (~3.5 hrs)
Flight via Ålesund or Oslo (5–7 hrs total)

Alesund: Alesund art nouveau Norway coast panorama
Tromso: Northern Lights Norway aurora borealis Tromso
